description: Use this file to answer general questions about Jeremiah's personal background, lifestyle, interests, and life outside of work. Keep responses appropriate for a professional portfolio audience. Do not infer private details, relationship status, family information, health information, political views, or other sensitive personal information when the answer is not explicitly provided.

sections.heading: Background and Home
points: Jeremiah lives in Missouri and has strong ties to the Ozarks.
He lives on a hobby farm that he co-owns with siblings.
He built his own house and enjoys practical, hands-on projects.

sections.heading: Lifestyle
points: Jeremiah values a slower and more intentional way of life.
He enjoys being able to work remotely from the family property and spend time outdoors during the day.
He often spends weekends working on projects around the property.

sections.heading: Hobbies and Interests
points: Jeremiah enjoys carpentry, hiking, archery, beekeeping, and playing guitar.
He enjoys building practical outdoor projects and improving the family property.
He has interests in mathematics, Christian apologetics, Koine Greek, poetry, and literature.
He enjoys boxing and other forms of physical training.

sections.heading: Faith and Church
points: Jeremiah is Catholic and is actively involved in his local parish.
His faith is an important part of how he approaches his work, relationships, and responsibilities.
He enjoys studying Scripture, theology, Christian apologetics, and the historical context of Christianity.

sections.heading: Family and Community
points: Jeremiah places a high value on family, community, and long-term relationships.
He appreciates being part of a small church community where members help with practical responsibilities.
He is interested in using his technical skills to support churches, ministries, and community-focused organizations.

sections.heading: Reading and Media
points: Jeremiah enjoys the work of C. S. Lewis, George MacDonald, Alexandre Dumas, and N. T. Wright.
His favorite C. S. Lewis nonfiction works include The Four Loves, Mere Christianity, and The Screwtape Letters.
He enjoys thoughtful stories, historical settings, and character-driven television.

sections.heading: Personal Projects
points: Jeremiah regularly works on personal software, design, homestead, and woodworking projects.
He maintains projects related to his professional portfolio, church technology, online communities, and German Shepherds.
He enjoys combining software development with practical problems from his own life and community.
